sections.title: A: Gap Fill (Book Review)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Seldom (1) ...... a reader encounter such an incisive examination of urban inequality as Marcus Webb's Divided Cities.
Those who choose to bury their heads in the (2) ...... and pretend that housing inequality is no longer an issue will find Webb's data deeply unsettling.
Webb persuasively demonstrates that (3) ...... only have those (4) ...... power consolidated their influence, but lower-income communities are also less able to stand (5) ...... to developers.
At (6) ...... stage in the book (7) ...... Webb's assertions made without supporting data, and at no point does he talk (8) ...... to readers unfamiliar with the topic.
His overarching argument, that (9) ...... when citizens unite and challenge the powers that (10) ...... will meaningful reform become possible, is both compelling and timely.

sections.title: B: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Repeated exposure to cold temperatures can ...... the skin, making it more resistant to minor abrasions. **HARD**
The coach's dedication to fair play ...... the values that the entire academy strives to uphold. **EXAMPLE**
Several volunteers felt ...... after the funding for the community project was unexpectedly withdrawn. **MORAL**
Victims of online fraud often describe a lasting feeling of ...... when they realise they cannot recover their money. **POWER**
There is an ongoing debate about whether it would be beneficial to ...... certain minor traffic offences. **CRIME**
A single vote might appear ...... in a national election, yet the collective impact of voter turnout is enormous. **SIGNIFY**
The security guard was dismissed after striking a customer without any apparent ...... . **PROVOKE**
After years of internal conflict, the region had become virtually ...... and required international intervention. **GOVERN**

sections.title: C: Collocations / Prepositions
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: She avoids checking her phone during dinner ...... a rule, preferring to focus on conversation.
Am I right ...... thinking that the conference has been moved to a different venue this year?
The merger will proceed, but it remains subject ...... approval by the financial regulatory body.
He ...... his time for the offence and was determined to rebuild his life upon release.
All confidential documents are kept ...... lock and key in the archive room on the ground floor.
The court reminded the defendant that no one, regardless of status, is ...... the law.
After months of negotiations, the militant faction agreed to lay ...... their weapons and enter peace talks.
The director decided to ...... an example of the employee who had leaked confidential information.

sections.title: D: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
There are no circumstances in which volunteers may enter the restricted zone without prior clearance. **under**
Volunteers ...... the restricted zone without prior clearance.
items.answer: are under no circumstances to enter

items.sentence:
I would advise against confronting the director over this issue. **horns**
I would advise against ...... the director over this issue.

The delegates could only vote once the chairperson had formally opened the session. **after**
Only ...... vote.
items.answer: after the chairperson had formally opened the session could the delegates

items.sentence:
The system would not let the students view their individual results. **denied**
The system ...... their individual results.

The fire alarm went off as soon as the technician entered the building. **had**
No ...... the fire alarm went off.
items.answer: sooner had the technician entered the building than

items.sentence:
A trustworthy insider informed me that the factory is about to close permanently. **authority**
I ...... that the factory is about to close permanently.

There was such widespread anger at the verdict that protests broke out across the city. **so**
Protests broke out across the city, ...... the verdict.
items.answer: so widespread was the anger at

items.sentence:
Why do you think you can speak to your colleagues in that manner? **gives**
What do you think ...... to speak to your colleagues in that manner?
The new planning regulations have generated considerable ...... among residents and local business owners alike.
This neighbourhood struggles with anti-social behaviour, ...... do several other districts in the region.
Any individual found tampering with public infrastructure will be ...... to the full extent of the law.
The transport authority has announced plans to ...... down on fare evasion across the entire network.
If the product arrives damaged, you have ...... right to request a full refund from the retailer.
Sofia is not the type to back down easily and will not let her supervisor push her ...... .
The ...... I find hard to grasp is why the committee approved the proposal without consulting residents first.
Fortunately, a well-connected mentor pulled a few ...... and helped her secure a placement at a top law firm.
